title: ESCALATE: Shell injection via filename in polyglot script

text: {"type":"escalate","finding":"Shell injection via filename in polyglot script","severity":"Important","description":"In tmux-claude-status:22, $pidfile is interpolated into a Python string literal. If a Claude session file has a name containing a single quote, the Python command breaks out of the string. Exploitation requires write access to ~/.claude/sessions/ (attacker already has user privileges), but this is a code quality concern.","options":[{"label":"Option 1: Pass filename via sys.argv","solution":"Change to: python3 -c \"import json,sys; print(json.load(open(sys.argv[1]))['pid'])\" \"$pidfile\"","pros":"Eliminates injection surface entirely, minimal change","cons":"Slight syntax change"},{"label":"Option 2: Use jq instead of python3","solution":"pid=$(jq -r '.pid' \"$pidfile\" 2>/dev/null)","pros":"Simpler, faster, no injection risk","cons":"Adds jq as a dependency (not currently required)"}],"recommendation":"Option 1 — no new dependency, clean fix","promotion_reason":"Natural FIX promoted to ESCALATE — max fix cycles (3) reached after 4 cycles"}

Acceptance (fix-cycle-5, T1.1 combined with TS-31):
- [ ] Lines 22 and 27 (pidfile reads) pass the path via `sys.argv` instead of string interpolation
- [ ] No occurrence of `open('$pidfile')` or `open("$pidfile")` remains in the file
- [ ] The script remains executable and `bash -n scripts/tmux-claude-status` exits 0
Note: This story is combined with TS-31 into task T1.1 (both edit tmux-claude-status)
